  <narrative>Created KPI-Dashboard.md as the central operational dashboard for Columbia Transport in the 2-Areas folder. The dashboard combines static baseline data (2025 personnel cost of EUR 905,260.73 with <35% revenue target, placeholders for MOL calculations) with dynamic capabilities requiring Dataview plugin installation. Three 2026 budget scenarios are documented (10%, 12%, 15% growth) with the structural decision to exclude customs duties. Service mix targets are visualized using ASCII bar charts across five service types (MAWB, AWB, FCL, LCL, crosstrade). The dashboard's power comes from three automated Dataview queries: (1) top 10 clients sorted by 2025 revenue showing MOL% and primary service, (2) service-level analysis aggregating shipment counts, total revenue, and average MOL sorted by profitability, and (3) active projects from 1-Projects folder with status and estimated value. Bidirectional links connect to detailed analysis pages for margins, clients, textile projects, and the AnalisiMarginalita agent, creating a knowledge graph that enables visual exploration of business relationships and performance patterns.</narrative>

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Created Service-Level Margin Analysis with Industry Benchmarks and Strategic Priorities</title>
  <subtitle>Documented MOL framework and detailed profitability analysis for MAWB, AWB, FCL, and LCL services with development rankings</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Marginalita-Servizi.md established MOL calculation framework: shipment MOL = revenue minus variable direct costs excluding personnel, margin % = MOL/revenue * 100</fact>
    <fact>MAWB correspondent service profiled: 15-25% margin (vs 12-20% sector), low risk back-to-back, action to negotiate better splits with top 3 correspondents</fact>
    <fact>AWB direct service profiled: 20-35% margin (vs 18-30% sector), medium risk with direct credit exposure, action to buy capacity blocks on stable routes</fact>
    <fact>FCL sea service profiled: 8-18% margin (vs 8-15% sector), high working capital with 30-90 day cycle, action to increase annual contracts versus spot rates</fact>
    <fact>LCL consolidation profiled: 18-30% margin (vs 15-25% sector), high operational complexity, optimal for small/medium textile clients with recurring shipments</fact>
    <fact>Strategic priority ranking established: #1 LCL textile recurring (high MOL, low risk, high loyalty), #2 AWB direct, #3 FCL crosstrade, #4 MAWB correspondent optimization</fact>

  <narrative>Created comprehensive service-level margin analysis document in 2-Areas/Columbia-Transport providing strategic intelligence for profitability optimization. The analysis establishes a clear MOL calculation framework distinguishing between shipment-level contribution margin (revenue minus variable costs excluding personnel structure costs) and company-level MOL (sum of shipments minus fixed costs). Four service types are profiled with industry benchmarks and actionable recommendations: MAWB correspondent services (15-25% margin, low risk, optimize commission splits), AWB direct (20-35% margin, higher risk requiring carrier relationship development and capacity block purchasing), FCL sea freight (8-18% margin with compressed pricing but high volume, requiring shift from spot to annual contracts for stability), and LCL consolidation (18-30% margin with high operational complexity but excellent for textile client retention). The strategic priority ranking places LCL textile recurring shipments first due to the combination of high margins, low risk, and strong customer loyalty economics, followed by AWB direct development, FCL crosstrade volume growth, and MAWB commission optimization. Bidirectional links connect to the KPI dashboard, market detail pages, and automated agent analysis reports, while a Dataview query enables tracking of historical MOL analysis evolution.</narrative>

  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Created Client Dashboard with Segmentation and Churn Risk Tracking</title>
  <subtitle>Built client registry with three automated Dataview queries for segmentation, churn prediction, and top performers by MOL</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Dashboard-Clienti.md created in 2-Areas/Clienti with instructions to create individual client notes using Template-Cliente</fact>
    <fact>Client segmentation defined: Textile/Fashion (Asia import/Europe export, LCL/AWB services), Manufacturing (machinery/components, FCL/MAWB), Crosstrade (intermediation, FCL/MAWB)</fact>
    <fact>Query 1 displays all active clients sorted by revenue showing segment, primary service, 2025 revenue, MOL%, and status</fact>
    <fact>Query 2 identifies churn-risk clients with MOL below 10% sorted by last shipment date to prioritize retention efforts</fact>
    <fact>Query 3 ranks top 5 clients by MOL% profitability with revenue and segment context</fact>

  <narrative>Created comprehensive client management dashboard in 2-Areas/Clienti enabling data-driven relationship tracking and risk management. The system uses structured YAML frontmatter in individual client notes to power three automated Dataview queries. Client segmentation maps three business verticals to their preferred services: textile/fashion clients favor LCL and direct AWB for Asia-Europe flows, manufacturing clients use FCL and MAWB for machinery shipments, and crosstrade intermediaries primarily use FCL and MAWB. The first query provides a complete active client roster sorted by revenue with profitability visibility. The second query implements proactive churn management by surfacing clients with sub-10% MOL sorted by recency of last shipment, enabling early intervention on at-risk relationships before they go dormant. The third query highlights the top 5 most profitable clients by MOL percentage, helping focus retention efforts on high-value relationships. The template standardizes client note creation with fields capturing segment classification, status lifecycle (active/dormant/lost), primary service, financial metrics, last activity date, and country of origin, creating a structured knowledge graph that links to the main KPI dashboard and service margin benchmarks.</narrative>

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Created Textile Logistics Strategic Project Hub with 2026 Targets and Risk Analysis</title>
  <subtitle>Established project index tracking textile vertical development with four KPI targets and automated client/route queries</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Project index _INDEX.md created in 1-Projects/LogisticaTessile with high priority status and strategic objective to increase recurring LCL share with loyal textile clients</fact>
    <fact>Four 2026 objectives defined: map all textile clients with individual MOL, identify profitable China-Italy LCL routes, negotiate annual contracts with top 3 by volume, develop 2 new textile clients in Q1-Q2</fact>
    <fact>Four KPI targets set: 15+ active textile clients, >60% LCL share of textile sea freight, >22% average MOL on textile LCL, >40% textile revenue as percentage of total</fact>
    <fact>Query 1 displays active textile clients from Clienti area sorted by revenue showing MOL%, primary service, and last shipment date</fact>
    <fact>Query 2 displays textile-tagged routes from Mercati resources sorted by CBM volume with average MOL and frequency metrics</fact>
    <fact>Project insights documented: strong seasonality with August-October import peak for Fall/Winter collections, textile clients prefer LCL for flexibility, fashion urgency air margins exceed 30%</fact>
    <fact>Three risks identified: China import geographic concentration, 2026 customs/tariff changes monitoring, aggressive competition from major players Kuehne+Nagel and Panalpina</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Created strategic project hub for textile logistics vertical development in 1-Projects/LogisticaTessile, representing Columbia Transport's core business growth initiative. The project aims to increase recurring LCL business with loyal textile/fashion clients through four concrete 2026 objectives: comprehensive client MOL mapping, identification of profitable China-Italy LCL routes, annual contract negotiation with the top 3 volume clients, and acquisition of 2 new textile clients in the first half of 2026. Success metrics are defined through four KPI targets achieving 15+ active clients, over 60% LCL modal share in textile sea freight (vs FCL), maintaining above 22% average MOL on textile LCL shipments, and growing textile to over 40% of total company revenue. Two Dataview queries provide dynamic visibility into the textile client portfolio (sorted by revenue with profitability and recency metrics) and active textile routes (sorted by volume with margin and frequency data). Project intelligence captures critical operational patterns: strong seasonality driven by August-October import peaks for Fall/Winter fashion collections, client preference for LCL due to flexibility and frequency needs, and opportunity in fashion urgency air freight with margins exceeding 30%. Risk assessment identifies three concerns: geographic concentration on China imports creating supply chain vulnerability, need to monitor 2026 customs/tariff changes (though elimination already factored into budget), and competitive pressure from major freight forwarders like Kuehne+Nagel and Panalpina aggressively targeting the textile vertical.</narrative>

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Created Sea LCL Market Intelligence Resource with Route and Margin Analysis</title>
  <subtitle>Documented LCL service structure, three primary routes, margin breakdown, and textile-driven seasonality patterns</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Sea-LCL.md created in 3-Resources/Mercati with frontmatter defining 22% average MOL, placeholders for shipment count and total revenue</fact>
    <fact>Service structure documented: Client to Columbia Transport (HAWB/HBL) to NVOCC Partner (MBL) to carrier, with margin earned on client vs NVOCC rate difference</fact>
    <fact>Three primary routes defined: China-Italy (Shanghai/Ningbo/Guangzhou to Genova/La Spezia, weekly, 28-35 days), India-Italy (JNPT/Mundra to Genova, twice weekly, 25-30 days), Turkey-Italy (Istanbul to Genova, weekly, 8-12 days)</fact>
    <fact>Margin component breakdown: ocean freight EUR 80-200/CBM (volatile spot), ocean margin 15-30 EUR/CBM negotiated with NVOCC, port duties pass-through plus 10%, handling pass-through plus 15%</fact>
    <fact>Target MOL range 18-28% with objective above 22% aligned to project targets</fact>
    <fact>Seasonality mapped: high peak July-October for textile Fall/Winter China imports, medium peak January-March for Spring/Summer collections, low season November-December and April-June</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Created market intelligence resource for Sea LCL (Less than Container Load) service in 3-Resources/Mercati, documenting Columbia Transport's core textile business offering. The service structure flows from client to Columbia Transport (issuing HAWB/HBL house bill) to NVOCC consolidation partner (holding MBL master bill) to ocean carrier, with Columbia's margin derived from the spread between client rates and NVOCC rates, exposing the company to both client credit risk and NVOCC partner reliability risk. Three primary trade lanes are mapped with operational parameters: China-Italy serving Shanghai/Ningbo/Guangzhou origins to Genova/La Spezia with weekly sailings and 28-35 day transit (the main textile import corridor), India-Italy from JNPT/Mundra to Genova with twice-weekly frequency and 25-30 day transit, and Turkey-Italy from Istanbul to Genova with weekly service and faster 8-12 day transit. Margin economics are detailed through component breakdown showing volatile ocean freight rates of EUR 80-200 per CBM on spot market, negotiated ocean margin of 15-30 EUR/CBM with NVOCC partners, and pass-through fees with 10% markup on port duties and 15% on handling, yielding target average MOL of 18-28% with the project objective above 22%. Seasonality is strongly driven by textile collections with high peak July-October for Fall/Winter imports from China, medium peak January-March for Spring/Summer, and low season in November-December and April-June between collection cycles. Links connect to FCL alternative, the strategic textile project, and comparative margin analysis across services.</narrative>

    <concept>trade-off</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
